description: Security rules for Go applications covering SQL injection, command injection, XSS, path traversal, SSRF, weak cryptography, JWT vulnerabilities, insecure network, and credential management

security.name: Go Security Rules
security.description: 22 security rules for Go applications covering CRITICAL and HIGH severity vulnerabilities

sql-injection.name: Go SQL Injection Rules
sql-injection.description: SQL injection detection for database/sql (GO-SEC-001), pgx (GO-SQLI-002), sqlx (GO-SQLI-003), and GORM (GO-GORM-SQLI-001, GO-GORM-SQLI-002)

xss.name: Go XSS Rules
xss.description: Cross-Site Scripting detection via unsafe html/template type conversions (GO-XSS-001), fmt.Fprintf (GO-XSS-002), and io.WriteString (GO-XSS-003)

crypto.name: Go Cryptography Rules
crypto.description: Weak cryptography detection: MD5 (GO-CRYPTO-001), SHA1 (GO-CRYPTO-002), DES (GO-CRYPTO-003), RC4 (GO-CRYPTO-004), MD5 for passwords (GO-CRYPTO-005)

injection.name: Go Injection Rules
injection.description: Command injection (GO-SEC-002), SSRF via resty (GO-SSRF-001), SSRF via net/http (GO-SSRF-002), path traversal (GO-PATH-001), open redirect (GO-REDIRECT-001)

network.name: Go Network Security Rules
network.description: Insecure network: HTTP without TLS (GO-NET-001), gRPC without TLS (GO-NET-002)

jwt.name: Go JWT Security Rules
jwt.description: JWT vulnerability detection: ParseUnverified skips signature validation (GO-JWT-002)

secrets.name: Go Secrets and Credentials Rules
secrets.description: Hardcoded credential detection: credential-named variables flowing into function calls (GO-SEC-004)
